title: Read a Receipt
summary: Check a purchase receipt for accuracy and understand what each line means.
learnerPromise: You will be able to read a store receipt line by line and spot mistakes.
whyItMatters: Receipts help you confirm you were charged the correct amount and give you proof of purchase if you need to return something.
realLifeUses: Noticing you were charged twice for the same item at a grocery store
Confirming a discount was applied before leaving the shop
Checking that the items listed match what you put in your bag
Keeping a receipt for a later warranty claim or exchange
materialsNeeded: A recent paper or digital receipt
result: You can explain each part of a receipt and identify one common error.
youWillLearn: Locate the date, store name, and transaction number on a receipt
Match each item to its price and quantity
Spot extra charges, discounts, and sales tax
Calculate the total to verify it is correct
miniChallenge: Grab a recent receipt and check whether the total matches the items you bought.
steps: Find a receipt from a recent purchase (paper or digital).
Look at the top for the store name, date, and time of purchase.
Read down the list of items and match each one to its price and quantity.
Look for subtotal, tax, discounts, and the final total.
Add up the item prices mentally or with a calculator to check the total.
completionCriteria: You can name the five main sections of a receipt
You found at least one detail you had not noticed before
commonProblems: Receipts with very small print or confusing abbreviations
Discounts that appear after the total instead of beside the item
Being charged for an item you did not pick up
tips: Keep receipts for expensive items until the return window passes
Take a photo of paper receipts so you have a backup
If something looks wrong, ask the store before you leave
